event_headline: OSG urges Supreme Court to deny Sen. Dela Rosa's bid to block ICC warrant, citing justice for EJK victims, as police prepare for pursuit.

chronology.action: Reported leaving the Senate with a PNP escort while wanted by the ICC
Submitted its comment on two petitions filed by Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court
Argued before the Supreme Court that justice should not forget the “voiceless dead” and urged the denial of petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
Asked the Supreme Court to dismiss the petition of Sen. Ronald dela Rosa to block the implementation of the ICC arrest warrant against him

chronology.actor: Former President Rodrigo Duterte's new lawyer
chronology.action: Requested a re-examination of his health and capacity to face trial before setting a date for the start of the ICC trial
Eyes 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in Duterte trial, with over 13,000 evidence still pending review
Rejected former President Rodrigo Duterte’s request to appeal the confirmation of charges against him
Tracker teams are now ready to pursue Senator Ronald dela Rosa
Stated they received information indicating that several more individuals may soon face arrest warrants from the ICC
Insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer

chronology.actor: Atty. Israelito Torreon (Sen. Dela Rosa's lawyer)
chronology.action: Stood firm that it is wrong to call his client, Sen. Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa, a “fugitive from justice”
Called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a "pro-life campaign"
Told Senator Bato dela Rosa to think about the families of alleged extrajudicial killing victims before invoking human rights and due process
Cited a higher risk of flight and possible obstruction of justice in ordering the continued detention of former President Rodrigo Duterte in The Hague
Ruled that former president Rodrigo Duterte will remain in detention in The Hague ahead of his trial for crimes against humanity

chronology.actor: Atty. Kristina Conti (ICC Assistant to Counsel, legal counsel of EJK victims)
chronology.action: Was reported to be live on DZBB
Made a 'bad joke' about his father already having 'company' in case Sen. Bato dela Rosa gets arrested by the ICC
Called on Senator [Dela Rosa] to consider voluntarily surrendering in connection with an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court (ICC)

key_actors.name: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
key_actors.role: Senator, former PNP chief, subject of an ICC arrest warrant
key_actors.stated_position: Filed petitions before the Supreme Court to block the implementation of the ICC arrest warrant against him; his lawyer states it is wrong to call him a 'fugitive from justice'.

key_actors.name: Office of the Solicitor General (OSG)
key_actors.stated_position: Urged the Supreme Court to deny Sen. Dela Rosa's petitions to block the ICC warrant, asserting that law must prevail for justice for the deceased.

key_actors.name: Supreme Court
key_actors.role: Highest judicial body in the Philippines
key_actors.stated_position: Receiving petitions from Sen. Dela Rosa and comments from the OSG regarding the ICC warrant.

key_actors.name: International Criminal Court (ICC)
key_actors.role: International judicial body investigating alleged crimes against humanity
key_actors.stated_position: Issued an arrest warrant, is prosecuting former President Duterte, and has rejected his appeal against charges.

key_actors.name: Philippine National Police (PNP)
key_actors.stated_position: Escorted Sen. Dela Rosa and has tracker teams ready to pursue him in connection with the ICC warrant.

key_actors.name: Former President Rodrigo Duterte
key_actors.role: Former President of the Philippines, subject of ICC trial
key_actors.stated_position: His lawyer requested a re-examination of his health; his request to appeal charges was rejected by the ICC; he remains in detention.

key_actors.name: NBI Director Melvin Matibag
key_actors.role: Director of the National Bureau of Investigation
key_actors.stated_position: Insisted that the ICC arrest warrant against Sen. Dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer; indicated more ICC warrants may be issued; called on Dela Rosa to surrender.

key_actors.name: Atty. Israelito Torreon
key_actors.role: Lawyer for Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa
key_actors.stated_position: Argued that it is wrong to call Sen. Dela Rosa a 'fugitive from justice' because the Supreme Court decision is provisional.

key_actors.name: Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano
key_actors.stated_position: Called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a 'pro-life campaign'.

key_actors.name: Atty. Lorna Kapunan
key_actors.stated_position: Urged Sen. Dela Rosa to consider the families of alleged extrajudicial killing victims before invoking human rights and due process.

key_actors.name: Davao City Mayor Baste Duterte
key_actors.role: Son of former President Rodrigo Duterte
key_actors.stated_position: Made a 'bad joke' suggesting his father already has 'company' in case Sen. Dela Rosa is arrested by the ICC.

key_actors.name: Atty. Kristina Conti
key_actors.role: ICC Assistant to Counsel and legal counsel of EJK victims
key_actors.stated_position: Discussed the status conference at the ICC regarding former President Duterte.

factual_claims.claim: The Office of the Solicitor General (OSG) submitted its comment on two petitions filed by Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa before the Supreme Court.
The OSG urged the Supreme Court to deny petitions of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa.
The OSG argued that justice should not forget the “voiceless dead” in its comment before the Supreme Court.
The OSG asserts that the law must prevail to ensure that justice is achieved for the deceased.
ICC prosecution eyes 20-30 witnesses to testify orally in Duterte trial.
Over 13,000 evidence is still pending review for the Duterte trial.
The International Criminal Court rejected former President Rodrigo Duterte’s request to appeal the confirmation of charges against him.
The Philippine National Police's tracker teams are now ready to pursue Senator Ronald dela Rosa.
NBI Director Melvin Matibag stated they received information indicating that several more individuals may soon face arrest warrants from the ICC.
NBI Director Melvin Matibag insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer.
Atty. Israelito Torreon is the lawyer for Sen. Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa.
Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano called the war on drugs of former President Rodrigo Duterte's administration a "pro-life campaign."
Atty. Kristina Conti is an ICC Assistant to Counsel and legal counsel of EJK victims.
Davao City Mayor Baste Duterte is the son of former President Rodrigo Duterte.
The ICC Trial Chamber cited a higher risk of flight and possible obstruction of justice in ordering the continued detention of former President Rodrigo Duterte in The Hague.
Former president Rodrigo Duterte will remain in detention in The Hague ahead of his trial for crimes against humanity before the International Criminal Court (ICC).

disputed_claims.claim: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa's actions placed him within the definition of fugitive.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: Atty. Israelito Torreon, Dela Rosa's lawyer, stated that it is wrong to call his client a “fugitive from justice,” explaining that the Supreme Court decision is merely a provisional one.

disputed_claims.claim: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa is a 'fugitive from justice'.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: Atty. Israelito Torreon, Dela Rosa's lawyer, stated that it is wrong to call his client a “fugitive from justice,” explaining that the Supreme Court decision is merely a provisional one.

disputed_claims.claim: The ICC arrest warrant against Sen. Bato dela Rosa could be served by a lawyer.
disputed_claims.competing_accounts: NBI Director Melvin Matibag insisted that the warrant of arrest against Sen. Bato dela Rosa cannot be served by a lawyer.

competing_frames.proponents: Office of the Solicitor General (OSG), Atty. Lorna Kapunan, Atty. Kristina Conti (ICC Assistant to Counsel, legal counsel of EJK victims), proponents of ICC warrant implementation.
competing_frames.summary: This frame emphasizes the need for justice for victims of alleged extrajudicial killings and advocates for holding officials accountable through legal processes, including the implementation of the ICC warrant.

competing_frames.frame: Defense of National Sovereignty / Resistance to ICC Jurisdiction
competing_frames.proponents: Sen. Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa (by filing petitions), his lawyer Atty. Israelito Torreon.
competing_frames.summary: This frame asserts national legal authority and questions the legitimacy or applicability of international bodies like the ICC over domestic matters and officials, seeking to prevent the implementation of international warrants.

competing_frames.frame: War on Drugs as a 'Pro-Life Campaign'
competing_frames.summary: This frame re-characterizes the Duterte administration's 'war on drugs' as a beneficial initiative aimed at protecting families and communities from the destructive effects of illegal drugs.
